tool_name: Sheet Metal Flat Pattern Development Calculator — Multi-Bend Blank Length and Bend Line Stack-Up Check
category: Sheet Metal / Press Brake / Flat Pattern Development
standards: DIN 6935 — Sheet metal — Bending, dimensions of bent parts
DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 2768-m — General tolerances — Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions
ISO 1101 — Geometrical product specifications (GPS) — Geometrical tolerancing
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production

inputs.name: Flat Pattern Development Method
inputs.hint: Flat pattern development method. BEND_ALLOWANCE_SUM: sum straight segments + total bend allowance. BEND_DEDUCTION_SUM: sum outside dimensions - total bend deduction. K_FACTOR_PER_BEND: computed from effective K-factor for all bends. CAM_TABLE_VERIFIED: use verified CAM bend table data

inputs.name: Dimension Convention
inputs.hint: Dimensioning convention used on the part print. OUTSIDE_VIRTUAL_SHARP: dimensions to virtual sharp intersections. INSIDE_TANGENT: dimensions to inside tangent points. CENTERLINE: dimensions to centerline. MIXED_REQUIRES_CONVERSION: mixed conventions — requires conversion to a single method

inputs.name: Bend Method
inputs.hint: Bending method used for all bends in the part. Affects K-factor range and minimum bend radius

inputs.name: Material Family
inputs.hint: Material family. Affects K-factor reference ranges and minimum bend radius recommendations

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Nominal material thickness. Sheet metal: 0.5-6 mm. Plate: 6-50 mm

inputs.name: Number of Bends
inputs.hint: Number of bends in the part. 1-12 bends supported. More than 12 bends: use iterative flat pattern development or CAM software

inputs.name: Number of Straight Segments
inputs.hint: Number of straight segments in the developed part. For a part with N bends: N+1 straight segments. Minimum 2 (one bend), maximum 13 (12 bends)

inputs.name: Sum of Straight Segment Lengths
inputs.hint: Total sum of all straight segment centerline lengths in the flat pattern. For outside virtual sharp convention: this excludes bend allowance contribution

inputs.name: Total Bend Sweep Angle
inputs.hint: Sum of all individual bend sweep angles. For 4 bends at 90° each: 360°. Maximum: 12 bends × 180° = 2160°
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle specification tolerance per angle sum

inputs.name: Average Inside Bend Radius
inputs.hint: Average inside bend radius across all bends. If radii vary, use length-weighted average. Typical: punch tip radius or V/6.67 for air bending
inputs.uncertainty_source: Radius gauge or average of individual bend measurements

inputs.name: Minimum Inside Bend Radius (any bend)
inputs.hint: The smallest inside radius among all bends in the part. Used for formability check

inputs.name: Minimum Permitted Inside Radius (material limit)
inputs.hint: Smallest inside radius permitted for the material per ISO 7438. If minimum_inside_radius_mm < minimum_permitted_inside_radius_mm, cracking risk exists

inputs.name: Effective K-Factor
inputs.hint: Effective K-factor for all bends (or average if K varies). Determined from coupon test, CAM table, or DIN 6935 reference. Used to compute bend allowance per bend
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material reference table or CAM bend table

inputs.name: K-Factor Source
inputs.hint: Source of the K-factor value. MEASURED_COUPON: highest confidence. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: lowest confidence

inputs.name: Total Known Bend Allowance (sum of all bends)
inputs.hint: Sum of known bend allowances for all bends. Optional — used when bend allowance data is available from CAM table or coupon test. Overrides K-factor calculation
inputs.uncertainty_source: CAM table or coupon test data

inputs.name: Total Known Bend Deduction (sum of all bends)
inputs.hint: Sum of known bend deductions for all bends. Optional — used with BEND_DEDUCTION_SUM method
inputs.uncertainty_source: CAM table or coupon test data

inputs.name: Sum of Outside Setbacks (all bends)
inputs.hint: Sum of all individual outside setback values. Optional — used for validation of flat pattern calculation
inputs.uncertainty_source: Calculated from individual bend geometry or CAM table

inputs.name: Blank Width (perpendicular to bend axis)
inputs.hint: Width of the blank perpendicular to the bend axis. Used for blank area calculation

inputs.name: Edge Trim Allowance per Side
inputs.hint: Additional material allowance per edge for post-bend trimming. Typical: 0.5-3 mm per side for clean edges

inputs.name: Saw or Laser Kerf Allowance
inputs.hint: Material removed by saw or laser cut per cut. Laser: 0.1-0.3 mm. Saw: 1-3 mm. Plasma: 1.5-3 mm

inputs.name: Grain Direction Relative to Bend Line
inputs.hint: Grain direction relative to the primary bend axis. ACROSS_GRAIN: preferred (bend line perpendicular to rolling direction). WITH_GRAIN: increases cracking risk

inputs.name: Bend Relief Provided
inputs.hint: Whether bend relief notches are provided at bend ends to prevent tearing. Recommended for flanges adjacent to bends

inputs.name: Minimum Flange Length in Part
inputs.hint: The shortest flange length in the part. Used for minimum flange length check

inputs.name: Minimum Required Flange Length
inputs.hint: Minimum flange length required for the given V-die opening (typically: V_die / 2 + flange clearance). Shorter flanges may not be formable
inputs.uncertainty_source: Die geometry and part design rule

inputs.name: Target Flat Length Tolerance
inputs.hint: Maximum acceptable deviation from the calculated flat pattern length. ISO 2768-m typical: ±0.2 mm precision, ±0.5 mm general

inputs.name: Length Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of straight segment length measurements

inputs.name: Thickness Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of thickness measurement

inputs.name: Radius Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of inside radius measurement

inputs.name: K-Factor Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of the K-factor. MEASURED_COUPON: ±0.005. SHOP_CAM_TABLE: ±0.01. DIN_6935_REFERENCE: ±0.02. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: ±0.05

inputs.name: Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of bend angle measurement per individual bend, summed across all bends
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or CNC position feedback calibration

formulas.name: Total Bend Angle in Radians
formulas.reference: ISO 80000 — Angle conversion: degrees to radians
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Radius to Thickness Ratio (minimum radius)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Ratio of minimum inside bend radius to material thickness
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Average Neutral Axis Radius
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Neutral axis radius: R_N = R_avg + K × t
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Calculated Total Bend Allowance (from K-factor)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 §4 — Total bend allowance: BA_total = θ_total × (R_avg + K × t). Sum of neutral axis arc lengths through all bends
assumptions: Average radius approximation for variable radii
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Effective Total Bend Allowance (method-dependent)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Effective bend allowance: uses measured/CAM data if available; otherwise K-factor calculated value
assumptions: Known bend allowance, when provided, overrides calculation
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Calculated Total Bend Deduction (from K-factor)
formulas.expression: calculated_total_bend_deduction_mm = 2 * bend_count * TAN((total_bend_sweep_angle_deg / MAX(bend_count, 1) * PI / 180) / 2) * (average_inside_radius_mm + material_thickness_mm) - effective_total_bend_allowance_mm
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 §6 — Total bend deduction: BD_total = sum of (2 × OSSB_i) - BA_total. Using average per-bend angle
assumptions: Uniform bend angle across all bends (average approximation)
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Effective Total Bend Deduction (method-dependent)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Effective bend deduction: uses known data if available; otherwise calculated value
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Developed Flat Length (Bend Allowance Sum Method)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Flat pattern length (bend allowance sum): L_flat = sum(L_straight) + total_BA
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Developed Flat Length (Bend Deduction Sum Method — validation)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Flat pattern length (bend deduction sum): L_flat = sum(L_outside) - total_BD
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Cut Length (flat pattern + trim + kerf)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Cut length: flat pattern length + edge trim allowance (both sides) + kerf allowance
assumptions: Linear addition of trim and kerf allowances
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Blank Area (approximate)
formulas.reference: Simple area calculation: area = length × width
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: K-Factor Confidence Score
formulas.expression: k_factor_confidence_score = k_factor_source == 'MEASURED_COUPON' ? 1.0 : k_factor_source == 'SHOP_CAM_TABLE' ? 0.9 : k_factor_source == 'MACHINE_TOOLING_TABLE' ? 0.8 : k_factor_source == 'DIN_6935_REFERENCE' ? 0.7 : k_factor_source == 'MATERIAL_SUPPLIER_DATA' ? 0.6 : k_factor_source == 'ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE' ? 0.3 : 0.5
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — K-factor confidence scoring by source
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Flat Length Uncertainty (RSS multi-bend)
formulas.expression: flat_length_uncertainty_mm = SQRT((length_measurement_uncertainty_mm)^2 + (bend_count * thickness_uncertainty_mm)^2 + (bend_count * radius_uncertainty_mm)^2 + (bend_count * k_factor_uncertainty)^2 + (angle_uncertainty_deg * (PI / 180) * (average_inside_radius_mm + effective_k_factor * material_thickness_mm))^2)
formulas.reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM — Combined standard uncertainty for multi-bend flat pattern using RSS method. Each bend contributes thickness, radius, and K-factor uncertainty
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Tolerance Utilization
formulas.reference: ISO 2768-m — Tolerance utilization: measurement uncertainty / target tolerance. Target: < 0.5 for reliable inspection
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Minimum Flange Utilization
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Flange utilization: actual flange length / required flange length. Target: >= 1.0 (flange is long enough)
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Combined Utilization Coefficient (UC) and Status Code
formulas.expression: UC = MAX(tolerance_utilization, 1 / MAX(minimum_flange_utilization, 0.001)). status_code = UC > 1.0 ? 'STACK_UP_UC_EXCEEDED' : UC > 0.8 ? 'STACK_UP_WARNING' : UC >= 0 ? 'STACK_UP_ACCEPTABLE' : 'ERROR'
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 / ISO 2768-m — Combined utilization coefficient considering both tolerance and flange length constraints
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

bend_count_valid.error_msg: Bend count must be between 1 and 12.

segment_count_valid.error_msg: Straight segment count must equal bend_count + 1 (range: 2-13).

straight_length_positive.error_msg: Sum of straight segment lengths must be positive.

total_angle_valid.error_msg: Total bend sweep angle must be between 0.1° and 2160° (max 12 bends × 180°).

avg_radius_positive.error_msg: Average inside bend radius must be positive.

k_factor_range.error_msg: Effective K-factor must be between 0 and 1.

flange_length_positive.error_msg: Both minimum and required flange lengths must be positive.

tolerance_positive.error_msg: Target flat length tolerance must be positive.

smart_warnings.message: STACK-UP UC EXCEEDED: The combined utilization coefficient exceeds 1.0. Either the tolerance margin is insufficient or the minimum flange length requirement is not met. Review part design or tolerance specification.
TOLERANCE MARGIN LOW: Multi-bend flat pattern uncertainty uses more than 80% of the tolerance band. Consider K-factor validation or tighter process control.
FLANGE LENGTH INADEQUATE: The minimum flange length is shorter than the required minimum for the given die opening. Flange may not be formable. Increase flange length or reduce V-die opening.
K-FACTOR VALIDATION REQUIRED: The K-factor source is an engineering estimate (confidence < 0.5). Perform a coupon test to validate K-factor before production, especially for multi-bend parts.
FLAT LENGTH WITHIN TOLERANCE: All checks passed. K-factor confidence high, tolerance margin adequate, flange lengths sufficient. Proceed with production.
INSIDE RADIUS BELOW MATERIAL LIMIT: The minimum inside bend radius is below the permitted limit. Risk of cracking in one or more bends. Consider using a larger radius.
BENDING WITH GRAIN AT LOW R/T RATIO: Multiple bends with grain direction at low R/t ratio significantly increases cracking risk. Consider rotating the blank or using a larger bend radius.
DEVELOPMENT METHOD MISMATCH: CAM_TABLE_VERIFIED method selected but K-factor confidence is below 0.8. Verify CAM bend table data matches the actual material and process conditions.
COMPLEX PART WITH LOW K-FACTOR CONFIDENCE: Part has more than 6 bends and the K-factor confidence is below 0.7. Accumulated uncertainty may be significant. Recommend physical flat pattern validation.
BEND RELIEF RECOMMENDED: Short flanges without bend relief are prone to tearing at bend ends. Consider adding bend relief notches.
NO EDGE TRIM ALLOWANCE: Edge trim allowance is set to 0. For critical dimension parts, consider adding trim allowance for post-bend edge finishing.

fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation
Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Flat pattern length likely out of tolerance or flange length inadequate.
Flange too short to form. Part geometry not achievable.
Flat pattern length may be incorrect for multi-bend part. Rework or scrap risk.
fmea.control_measure: DIN 6935 — Coupon test recommended before production

fmea.effect: Material cracking at one or more bend locations.
Accumulated bend uncertainty consumes most of the tolerance band.
Increased cracking risk at multiple bend locations.
fmea.control_measure: ISO 7438 — Grain direction check for all bends

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on zero/negative thickness, invalid bend count (1-12), segment count != bend_count+1, zero straight length, invalid total angle, invalid K-factor range, zero blank width, zero/negative flange lengths, zero tolerance.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: 2-bend simple channel part, 4-bend box part, 6-bend complex part, bend allowance sum method, bend deduction sum method, tolerance exceeded case, flange length inadequate case, K-factor confidence scoring, uncertainty RSS computation, combined UC > 1.0.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F16 in deterministic order.
Four development methods: BEND_ALLOWANCE_SUM, BEND_DEDUCTION_SUM, K_FACTOR_PER_BEND, CAM_TABLE_VERIFIED.
Total BA = sum of all individual bend allowances (K-factor or known).
Total BD = sum of all individual bend deductions.
Flat pattern (BA method) = sum straight segments + total BA.
Flat pattern (BD method) = sum outside dimensions - total BD.
Cut length = flat pattern + 2 × edge trim + kerf.
Blank area = cut length × blank width.
K-factor confidence scored 0.3-1.0 based on source.
Multi-bend uncertainty: thickness, radius, and K-factor uncertainties scale with sqrt(bend_count).
Angle uncertainty contribution scaled by total bend angle in radians.
All gate outputs are boolean or string. Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.
Minimum flange length check depends on V-die geometry approximation.
Bend relief boolean affects formability warning only (not calculation).

metadata.description: Multi-bend sheet metal flat pattern development calculator per DIN 6935. Computes developed flat length, total bend allowance, total bend deduction, cut length (including trim and kerf), blank area, K-factor confidence, tolerance utilization, minimum flange length check, and combined utilization coefficient (UC) for parts with 1-12 bends.
tuv_checklist: DIN 6935 flat pattern development methodology verified
ISO 7438 minimum bend radius check included
ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM uncertainty propagation implemented
Multi-bend bend allowance sum method validated
Multi-bend bend deduction sum method validated
Combined UC with tolerance and flange constraints verified
metadata.calibration_trace: Flat pattern calculations based on DIN 6935 neutral axis model. K-factor references traceable to DIN standard tables, CAM bend table data, and physical coupon tests. Uncertainty values per ISO 80000 and ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM. Multi-bend uncertainty accumulation modeled with bend_count scaling.
